title: User Input
description: Learn to talk to your users through the console with cin.

slides.title: Welcome cin! 🎙️
slides.content:
You already know how to output text using `cout`.

Now we will learn how to get **user input** using `cin` (stands for **c**haracter **in**put).

slides.title: Using cin
slides.content:
`cin` uses the **extraction operator** `>>`.

Here's an example of asking a user for their age:

quizData.question: Which operator is used with cin?
quizData.explanation: cout uses << (insertion operator) and cin uses >> (extraction operator).

slides.title: A Quick Chat 💬

quizData.question: You can use cin to grab both strings and integers from the user.
quizData.explanation: cin can read data into any variable type, including ints and strings.
